name: multiplation

phase_0.boundary_condition: The arithmetic logic unit (ALU) register width

VINIT.justification: The uninitialized accumulator state before any calculation begins.

TANCH.justification: The terminal boundary that defines the maximum representable value of the system.

AFWD.justification: The forward transformation of magnitude in binary multiplication.

AREV.justification: The descent/inverse operation used in division or partial product reduction.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of partial products into a derived sum.

IMSCRIB.justification: The value which, when multiplied, recognizes the input as itself.

FSPLIT.justification: Splitting a multiplier into its constituent power-of-two bits.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstituting the decomposed bits back into the final product magnitude.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the current bit position contributes to the sum.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the current bit position is skipped.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where a bit represents both addition and subtraction potential simultaneously.

IFIX.justification: The irreversible fixation of the final product into permanent storage.

phase_3.true_description: Logic High; bit is active, multiplication occurs.
phase_3.false_description: Logic Low; bit is inactive, zero-pass.
phase_3.both_description: Quantum superposition or Booth-encoded transition state.

steps.domain_action: Clear accumulator and load operands into registers.
Verify multiplicand against identity to establish base magnitude.
Decompose the multiplier into a sequence of boolean bit-tests.
If bit is 1, trigger the addition branch.
Shift multiplicand magnitude to align with the current power-of-two position.
Compose the shifted multiplicand into the running partial sum.
If bit is 0, bypass the addition logic.
Decrement the multiplier bit-counter to move to the next position.
Handle sign-bit parity where the value is held as both magnitude and direction.
Fuse all active partial products to recover the total product magnitude.
Commit the final result to the output register, ending the volatile calculation.
Seal the operation within the bit-width boundary and set completion flags.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The instruction decoder translating 'MUL' into micro-ops.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The internal data bus carrying partial sums between adders.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: L1 Cache storing the multiplicand during the shift-add cycle.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The clock signal triggering each bit-shift iteration.
phase_5.alfs_store: The hardware-level lookup table for small-integer primitives.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Heat dissipation from logic gate switching.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: High potential energy; operands separated in distinct registers.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Lower potential energy; information compressed into a single product register.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The logical operation is reversible in an idealized Fredkin gate, though physically dissipative.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → EVALF → AREV → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: multiplation
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 9)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def multiplation_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Clear accumulator and load operands into registers.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Verify multiplicand against identity to establish base magnitude.)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (Decompose the multiplier into a sequence of boolean bit-tests.) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (If bit is 1, trigger the addition branch.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Shift multiplicand magnitude to align with the current power-of-two position.)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the shifted multiplicand into the running partial sum.)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (If bit is 0, bypass the addition logic.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Decrement the multiplier bit-counter to move to the next position.)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Handle sign-bit parity where the value is held as both magnitude and direction.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Fuse all active partial products to recover the total product magnitude.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Commit the final result to the output register, ending the volatile calculation.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the operation within the bit-width boundary and set completion flags.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def multiplation_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (multiplation_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def multiplation_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (multiplation_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem multiplation_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
